title: Teaching
text:
PhD Workshops

Applied Language Models for Social Science Research ( Github)

Five-hour workshop on the application of language models to social science research aimed at graduate students and researchers with prior experience in Python and basic machine learning.

Causal Inference Using Observational Data ( Github)

Five-hour workshop intended to provide an introduction to causal inference methods for observational data. Workshop is geared towards graduate students and researchers with prior experience in statistics and coding in R.

Data Collection Using Public APIs ( Github)

This workshop will equip students with the skills to programmatically access data from public REST APIs, transform this data into analysis-ready datasets, and critically consider dataset design for social science research. A working understanding of Python is recommended.

title: Zach Dickson
text:
Thanks for stopping by! I’m a Postdoctoral Fellow in Quantitative Methods in the Department of Methodology at the London School of Economics. I’m also affiliated with the Data Science Institute, the Grantham Research Institute on Climate Change and the Environment, and the Public Opinion Analytics Lab. I completed my PhD at the University of Glasgow in 2024.
My research is broadly situated in political behavior, with specific interests in right-wing populism and climate politics. I’m a big fan of research methods, and most of my work includes either computational or causal inference methods. I’m also always on the prowl for new sources of data to better understand social behavior, and my past work has relied on data from social media, newspapers, governments, political parties and many others.
My research has been published in Political Communication, Comparative Political Studies, the American Political Science Review, among other journals. My research has also been covered in The Economist, The New Statesman, The Guardian, Forbes, El País, and in various blogs highlighted on my Research page.
For more on my research, check out my Research page or my Google Scholar page. My CV is also available here.
